title: Flavorful Canadian Baby Aubergine Skillet
description: A weeknight Canadian dish featuring baby aubergine with a shio-koji cure, this recipe is low-fat-friendly and perfect for a quick dinner. The briny and aerated flavors will leave you wanting more.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced baby aubergine and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, until tender and lightly browned.
Remove the aubergine from the skillet and set aside.
Add the minced garlic and chopped onion to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the shio-koji, chicken broth, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Return the cooked aubergine to the skillet and toss to coat with the shio-koji mixture.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes, until the flavors have melded together.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
